COR.PORATE PARALEGAII

KATI{Y MTCI]EL
PT,IBUC AFFAIRS PARALEGAL
A. BROC( EDMI'NDS
October 20, 1992
Ms. Maryann Kvasager
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
RE: POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEES - OUALIFICATIONS
Dear Ms. Kvasager:
Thank you for taking the time to speak with me today regarding the
requirements for qualifying as a political action committee in the State
of California.
ln our conversation, we discussed parts (a) and (b) of g 82013 of the
Political Reform Act (definition of a 'committee"). Based upon our
conversation, it is my understanding that because our unofficial "PAC" has
never received more than $1,000.00 in contributions during a calendar
year, we do not need to file a Statement of Organization (CA 1991 Form
410). lt is also my understanding that our unofficial 'PAC'may make
contributions to political candidates without filing Form 4'10, as long as
the total expenditures is less than g'l ,000.00 per calendar year.
Please contact me if what I have concluded from of our conversation
is incorrect in any way. My extension is 43232. Thanks again for your
ass ista nce.
Sincerely,
A ru''''nA*",g
A. Brock Edmunds
Paralegal I
g ,-. i 'tZ

sections.question_synthetic: An individual is seeking clarification on whether an unofficial PAC that has never received more than $1,000 in contributions during a calendar year must file a Statement of Organization (Form 410) and whether it can make contributions to political candidates without filing if expenditures remain below $1,000 per calendar year.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence letter, not an FPPC advice letter response. The document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. It appears to be a follow-up confirmation from a requestor after a phone conversation with FPPC staff. The paralegal is seeking confirmation of informal guidance received verbally.

Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804 RE: POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEES - OUALIFICATIONS Dear Ms. Kvasager: Thank you for taking the time to speak with me today regarding the requirements for qualifying as a political action committee in the State of California. ln our conversation, we discussed parts (a) and (b) of g 82013 of the Political Reform Act (definition of a 'committee"). Based upon our conversation, it is my understanding that because our unofficial "PAC" has never received more than $1,000.00 in contributions during a calendar year, we do not need to file a Statement of Organization (CA 1991 Form 410). lt is also my understanding that our unofficial 'PAC'may make contributions to political candidates without filing Form 4'10, as long as the total expenditures is less than g'l ,000.00 per calendar year. embedding.summary: This is an incoming correspondence from A. Brock Edmunds, a paralegal, to the FPPC thanking them for a phone conversation and confirming his understanding that an unofficial PAC with less than $1,000 in annual contributions does not need to file Form 410 and may make candidate contributions without filing as long as expenditures stay below $1,000 annually.
